title: Plot a gg_variable object, — plot.gg_variable
code: ## ------------------------------------------------------------ ## classification ## ------------------------------------------------------------ ## -------- iris data set.seed(42) rfsrc_iris <- randomForestSRC::rfsrc(Species ~ ., data = iris, ntree = 50)  gg_dta <- gg_variable(rfsrc_iris) plot(gg_dta, xvar = "Sepal.Width") #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'  plot(gg_dta, xvar = "Sepal.Length") #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'   ## Panel plot across all predictors plot(gg_dta,   xvar = rfsrc_iris$xvar.names,   panel = TRUE, se = FALSE ) #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `se`   ## ------------------------------------------------------------ ## regression ## ------------------------------------------------------------ ## -------- air quality data # na.action = "na.impute" handles missing Ozone / Solar.R values set.seed(42) rfsrc_airq <- randomForestSRC::rfsrc(Ozone ~ ., data = airquality,                     na.action = "na.impute", ntree = 50) gg_dta <- gg_variable(rfsrc_airq)  # Treat Month as an ordinal factor for better visualization gg_dta[, "Month"] <- factor(gg_dta[, "Month"])  plot(gg_dta, xvar = "Wind") #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'  plot(gg_dta, xvar = "Temp") #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'  plot(gg_dta, xvar = "Solar.R") #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x' #> Warning: Removed 7 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_smooth()`). #> Warning: Removed 7 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).   # Factor variable uses notched boxplots plot(gg_dta, xvar = "Month", notch = TRUE) #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `notch` #> Notch went outside hinges #> ℹ Do you want `notch = FALSE`?   # \donttest{ # Panel plot across continuous predictors (loess smooths; slower) plot(gg_dta, xvar = c("Solar.R", "Wind", "Temp", "Day"), panel = TRUE) #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x' #> Warning: Removed 7 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range (`stat_smooth()`). #> Warning: Removed 7 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). #> Warning: Removed 7 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).  # }  ## ------------------------------------------------------------ ## survival examples ## ------------------------------------------------------------ ## -------- veteran data # \donttest{ data(veteran, package = "randomForestSRC") set.seed(42) rfsrc_veteran <- randomForestSRC::rfsrc(Surv(time, status) ~ ., veteran,   nsplit = 10,   ntree = 50 )  # Marginal survival at 90 days gg_dta <- gg_variable(rfsrc_veteran, time = 90)  # Single-variable dependence plots plot(gg_dta, xvar = "age") #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'  plot(gg_dta, xvar = "diagtime") #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'   # Panel coplot for two predictors at a single time plot(gg_dta, xvar = c("age", "diagtime"), panel = TRUE) #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'   # Compare survival at 30, 90, and 365 days simultaneously gg_dta <- gg_variable(rfsrc_veteran, time = c(30, 90, 365))  # Single-variable plot (one facet per time point) plot(gg_dta, xvar = "age") #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'   # Panel coplot across two predictors and three time points plot(gg_dta, xvar = c("age", "diagtime"), panel = TRUE) #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'  # }

code: if (requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) {   data(Boston, package = "MASS")   rfsrc_boston <- randomForestSRC::rfsrc(medv ~ ., Boston)    # To create 6 intervals, we want 7 points.   # quantile_pts will find balanced intervals   rm_pts <- quantile_pts(rfsrc_boston$xvar$rm, groups = 6, intervals = TRUE)    # Use cut to create the intervals   rm_grp <- cut(rfsrc_boston$xvar$rm, breaks = rm_pts)    summary(rm_grp) } #> (3.56,5.76] (5.76,5.99] (5.99,6.21] (6.21,6.44] (6.44,6.85] (6.85,8.78]  #>          85          84          84          85          84          84

title: Survival partial dependence data for one or more predictors — surv_partial.rfsrc
text: rforest fitted rfsrc survival competing-risk forest object. var_list Character vector predictor names partial dependence computed. must appear rforest$xvar.names. npts Integer; number predictor grid points evaluate (default 25). Evenly-spaced unique values sampled predictor. partial.type prediction type return. survival forests one "surv" (default), "mort", "chf". competing risk forests one "years.lost", "cif", "chf". See partial.rfsrc full details.

title: Survival partial dependence data for one or more predictors — surv_partial.rfsrc
text: named list one element per variable var_list. element list : name predictor variable name (character). dta raw output get.partial.plot.data, list containing minimum x (predictor values) yhat (partial predictions), survival/competing risk, partial.time.

title: Survival partial dependence data for one or more predictors — surv_partial.rfsrc
text: Computes partial dependence curves survival competing-risk rfsrc forest.  predictor calls partial.rfsrc npts evenly-spaced unique values, across every stored event time.

title: Survival partial dependence data for one or more predictors — surv_partial.rfsrc
code: ## ------------------------------------------------------------ ## survival ## ------------------------------------------------------------  data(veteran, package = "randomForestSRC") v.obj <- randomForestSRC::rfsrc(Surv(time,status)~.,   veteran, nsplit = 10, ntree = 100)  spart <- surv_partial.rfsrc(v.obj, var_list="age", partial.type = "mort") #> Warning: 'surv_partial.rfsrc' is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. #> Use 'gg_partial_rfsrc()' instead, which returns a classed object with a #> dedicated plot() method.  ## partial effect of age on mortality partial.obj <- randomForestSRC::partial(v.obj,                        partial.type = "mort",                        partial.xvar = "age",                        partial.values = v.obj$xvar$age,                        partial.time = v.obj$time.interest) pdta <- randomForestSRC::get.partial.plot.data(partial.obj)  plot(lowess(pdta$x, pdta$yhat, f = 1/3),      type = "l", xlab = "age", ylab = "adjusted mortality")   ## example where x is discrete - partial effect of age on mortality ## we use the granule=TRUE option partial.obj <- randomForestSRC::partial(v.obj,                        partial.type = "mort",                        partial.xvar = "trt",                        partial.values = v.obj$xvar$trt,                        partial.time = v.obj$time.interest) pdta <- randomForestSRC::get.partial.plot.data(partial.obj, granule = TRUE) boxplot(pdta$yhat ~ pdta$x, xlab = "treatment", ylab = "partial effect")    ## partial effects of karnofsky score on survival karno <- quantile(v.obj$xvar$karno) partial.obj <- randomForestSRC::partial(v.obj,                        partial.type = "surv",                        partial.xvar = "karno",                        partial.values = karno,                        partial.time = v.obj$time.interest) pdta <- randomForestSRC::get.partial.plot.data(partial.obj)  matplot(pdta$partial.time, t(pdta$yhat), type = "l", lty = 1,         xlab = "time", ylab = "karnofsky adjusted survival") legend("topright", legend = paste0("karnofsky = ", karno), fill = 1:5)    ## ------------------------------------------------------------ ## competing risk ## ------------------------------------------------------------  data(follic, package = "randomForestSRC") follic.obj <- randomForestSRC::rfsrc(Surv(time, status) ~ ., follic, nsplit = 3, ntree = 100)  ## partial effect of age on years lost partial.obj <- randomForestSRC::partial(follic.obj,                        partial.type = "years.lost",                        partial.xvar = "age",                        partial.values = follic.obj$xvar$age,                        partial.time = follic.obj$time.interest) pdta1 <- randomForestSRC::get.partial.plot.data(partial.obj, target = 1) pdta2 <- randomForestSRC::get.partial.plot.data(partial.obj, target = 2)  # Save and restore the user's graphical parameters per CRAN policy. oldpar <- par(no.readonly = TRUE) on.exit(par(oldpar)) par(mfrow = c(2, 2)) plot(lowess(pdta1$x, pdta1$yhat),      type = "l", xlab = "age", ylab = "adjusted years lost relapse") plot(lowess(pdta2$x, pdta2$yhat),      type = "l", xlab = "age", ylab = "adjusted years lost death")  ## partial effect of age on cif partial.obj <- randomForestSRC::partial(follic.obj,                        partial.type = "cif",                        partial.xvar = "age",                        partial.values = quantile(follic.obj$xvar$age),                        partial.time = follic.obj$time.interest) pdta1 <- randomForestSRC::get.partial.plot.data(partial.obj, target = 1) pdta2 <- randomForestSRC::get.partial.plot.data(partial.obj, target = 2)  matplot(pdta1$partial.time, t(pdta1$yhat), type = "l", lty = 1,         xlab = "time", ylab = "age adjusted cif for relapse") matplot(pdta2$partial.time, t(pdta2$yhat), type = "l", lty = 1,         xlab = "time", ylab = "age adjusted cif for death")

what: Recover original variable names from varpro one-hot encoded feature names — varpro_feature_names
title: Recover original variable names from varpro one-hot encoded feature names — varpro_feature_names
text: varpro one-hot encodes factor variables, appending numeric suffix level – sex becomes sex0 sex1.  map back, function strips suffix one character time every name varpro_names matches column dataset.

title: Recover original variable names from varpro one-hot encoded feature names — varpro_feature_names
code: varpro_feature_names(varpro_names, dataset)

title: Recover original variable names from varpro one-hot encoded feature names — varpro_feature_names
text: varpro_names character vector names output varpro (may include one-hot encoded suffixed names "sex0", "sex1") dataset original data frame passed varpro, used look valid column names

title: Recover original variable names from varpro one-hot encoded feature names — varpro_feature_names
text: character vector unique original variable names (suffixes)

title: Recover original variable names from varpro one-hot encoded feature names — varpro_feature_names
code: ## ------------------------------------------------------------------ ## Simple case: one continuous variable + one binary factor ## ------------------------------------------------------------------ ds <- data.frame(age = c(25, 30, 45), sex = c("M", "F", "M"))  # varpro one-hot encodes 'sex' into 'sex0' and 'sex1' varpro_names <- c("age", "sex0", "sex1") varpro_feature_names(varpro_names, ds) #> [1] "age" "sex" # Returns: c("age", "sex")  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------ ## Multi-level factor: three-level 'group' variable ## ------------------------------------------------------------------ ds2 <- data.frame(score = 1:6,                   group = factor(rep(c("A", "B", "C"), 2)))  # varpro appends 0/1/2 for each level vn2 <- c("score", "group0", "group1", "group2") varpro_feature_names(vn2, ds2) #> [1] "score" "group" # Returns: c("score", "group")  ## ------------------------------------------------------------------ ## Already-clean names pass through unchanged ## ------------------------------------------------------------------ ds3 <- data.frame(x = 1:5, y = 1:5) varpro_feature_names(c("x", "y"), ds3) #> [1] "x" "y" # Returns: c("x", "y")

title: ggRandomForests v3.5.0
text: plot.gg_varpro() longer draws phantom “NA” category nvar smaller number variables fit reports. $imp/$stats truncated nvar, per-tree overlay ($imp.tree) class-conditional data ($conditional) still carry every variable; re-levelling truncated $imp levels orphaned extras NA, rendered empty box/bar. rows now dropped, displayed variables appear. vignettes now render figures ragg quantise 256-colour palette, cutting source tarball 4.7 MB 2.3 MB. vignettes never chosen graphics device, fell default png(), writes RGBA truecolor: alpha channel opaque plots never use, tens thousands anti-aliased colours PNG compress. Figures visually unchanged (mean pixel difference 1.55 0-255 scale). steps build-time degrade -ops ragg magick absent, vignette rebuild without still succeeds – old file size. varPro vignette now documents variables varpro fit actually makes available. fit narrows predictors twice – object$xvar.names holds varPro::partialpro() can reach, varPro::get.topvars() reported ranking – partialpro() silently drops requested name outside first set. new section covers naming xvar.names get past reported ranking, split.weight = FALSE widen candidate set , two arguments (nvar, sparse) look like help don’t. gg_partial_varpro() now warns name passed xvar.names one varpro fit reach, instead letting disappear. partialpro() keeps names finds object$xvar.names says nothing rest, request twelve variables come back ten. check runs partialpro() , warning arrives ahead computation rather ; names every dropped variable points split.weight = FALSE. Supplying part_dta unchanged – variables already gone . function’s examples now cover object-driven path, none. gg_partial_varpro(scale = "chf") now computes variables name xvar.names instead every variable fit can reach. chf path routes gg_partial_rfsrc() rather partialpro(), never given variable list – asking one variable quietly work fourteen. mirror image partialpro() bug : one returns fewer variables asked , one returned . Naming variable forest carry always error still . partialpro-arguments (cut, nsmp) mean nothing path now ignored warning rather silence. Fix: gg_vimp() randomForest fit grown importance = TRUE now reports permutation importance asked . reporting node purity instead, silently: randomForest stores %IncMSE IncNodePurity side side, gg_vimp() stacked one vimp column ranked together. two commensurable – node purity runs thousands %IncMSE runs tens – every impurity row outranked every permutation row, truncation nvar cut permutation values away entirely. randomForest(medv ~ ., Boston, importance = TRUE) plot showed lstat = 12576.7 (node purity) permutation value lstat = 62.4. Node purity now left ranking; read randomForest::importance(object) want . Fits grown without importance = TRUE unaffected – ever stored node purity, still get. Fix: gg_vimp() randomForest classification fit grown importance = TRUE now reports permutation importance well. matrix mixes two scales – permutation column per class plus MeanDecreaseAccuracy, alongside MeanDecreaseGini – wider single-outcome branch picks one measure, skipped branch every column ranked together. MeanDecreaseGini came sole survivor: randomForest(Species ~ ., iris, importance = TRUE), gg_vimp() returned 4 rows node purity 16 rows permutation importance report. per-class columns MeanDecreaseAccuracy permutation measures one scale, now kept together named set column, way rfsrc fit’s /<class> columns already ; MeanDecreaseGini dropped. Fix: .outcome now selects column asked randomForest classification fit. .outcome = 0 documented overall importance took column 1 get , .outcome = k took column k + 1 class k. right rfsrc fit, whose $importance leads column, neither right : randomForest matrix opens classes keeps overall permutation measure MeanDecreaseAccuracy, near end. 0 returned first class labelled overall – randomForest(Species ~ ., iris, importance = TRUE) handed back setosa’s values, ranking Petal.Width Petal.Length overall measure way round – every class index shifted one, 1 giving versicolor. columns now resolved name: 0 reaches MeanDecreaseAccuracy, k reaches class k, .outcome = 1 agrees .outcome = "setosa". Fits grown importance = FALSE keep MeanDecreaseAccuracy column single measure answers 0 . .outcome now names measure selected set column, rfsrc randomForest fits. Asking one measure reported set literal "vimp" – pivot takes set source column name, selected column named vimp column written rather measure held. one path say measure want one path tell measure got. gg_vimp(rfsrc_iris, .outcome = 0) now reports set == "", gg_vimp(rf_iris, .outcome = 0) reports set == "MeanDecreaseAccuracy", agree names unfiltered pivot always used. Values ordering unchanged, plots unaffected: plot.gg_vimp() facets set one , selecting measure leaves exactly one. nvar counts variables randomForest fits, rows. applied multiclass pivot, frame holds one row per variable per measure, lopped whole measures end ranking instead trimming ranking . gg_vimp() now says ?gg_vimp randomForest fit without importance = TRUE stores IncNodePurity, ranking node purity rather permutation VIMP, nothing plot marks difference. example now passes importance = TRUE. gg_error() now explains error trajectory randomForestSRC’s record, : rfsrc()’s block.size defaults NULL unless request importance, stores error final tree , default fit gives gg_error() single point rather curve – tree.err = TRUE alone change . Grow block.size = 1 error every tree. examples now; plotting one dot. gg_beta_varpro(): imp column documented absolute coefficient. varPro::beta.varpro() wraps every coefficient returns abs(), sign discarded upstream never reaches us – docs said “Sign real (direction local association)”, read output. Use gg_ivarpro() signed local estimator. gg_isopro(): “’s output” section now says polarity flip . varPro::isopro()’s howbad lower = anomalous; return 1 - howbad higher = anomalous. section credited fit, contradicting function’s @return. Added gg_shap() plot.gg_shap() (shap_importance(), shap_beeswarm(), shap_dependence()) SHAP explanations regression classification forests, wrapping kernelshap (Suggests). gg_shap() now enforces integer contract bg_n .class instead silently coercing . documented integers, loosely checked: bg_n = 1.9 truncated 1 bg_n = Inf (value .Machine$integer.max) became NA, .class = 2.9 passed range check indexed column 2 – returning SHAP values class caller never asked . Non-whole, non-finite, --range non-scalar values now raise clear error. Valid input unaffected. Added print.gg_shap() summary.gg_shap(). gg_shap gg_* class without , dumped every row REPL instead showing header. print() now gives standard one-line header (variable observation counts) summary() returns summary.gg object reporting baseline, background-sample size, explained class classification fits, top variables mean |SHAP|. package help page (?ggRandomForests) now describes whole current surface – SHAP, Brier, varPro unsupervised-varPro families missing – longer claims plot() methods may return list ggplot2 objects; returns single plottable object (ggplot, patchwork composite multi-panel methods). gg_partial() longer lets survival partial dependence mistaken probability. randomForestSRC::plot.variable() defaults surv.type = "mort", yhat mortality – expected event count, value [0, 1] – superficially resembles percentage. yhat passed unscaled (rescaling corrupt quantity); instead label describing plotted carried object attr(x, "ylabel") used y-axis title plot.gg_partial(). Note gg_partial_rfsrc() defaults partial.type = "surv" report survival probabilities: two entry points report different quantities default. (#15)

title: ggRandomForests v3.4.1
text: remaining rfsrc/randomForest wrappers – gg_error(), gg_vimp(), gg_variable(), gg_rfsrc(), gg_brier() – now default S3 methods, wrong-class input gives clear “expected ‘rfsrc’ ‘randomForest’ object” error (naming class got) instead R’s generic “applicable method”. finishes dispatch-consistency pass started varPro family 3.4.0. (gg_roc() keeps existing gg_roc.rfsrc default, accepts rfsrc-shaped objects.)

title: ggRandomForests v3.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-02 gg_isopro(), gg_beta_varpro(), gg_ivarpro() now default S3 methods, wrong-class input gives clear “expected ‘’ object” error (naming class got) instead R’s generic “applicable method”. makes varPro-family wrappers consistent gg_beta_uvarpro() / gg_sdependent(); previously-unreachable inner class checks removed. Fix: gg_partial_rfsrc() now computes partial dependence correctly factor predictors. passing factor labels partial.values randomForestSRC::partial.rfsrc(), imposes level integer code (internally .numeric(partial.values)). Character labels (“”/“Yes”) became NA numeric-looking labels (“4”/“6”/“8”) became --range codes, every level collapsed single value (flat categorical partial plot). wrapper now passes integer codes relabels output, matching plot.variable(partial = TRUE) ground-truth partial dependence. categorical x now returned factor model’s level order, plot keeps order instead re-sorting alphabetically. Continuous numeric low-cardinality predictors unaffected. gg_beta_uvarpro() / plot.gg_beta_uvarpro(): tidy wrapper bar chart varPro::get.beta.entropy() – unsupervised analogue gg_beta_varpro(). uvarpro() fit aggregates per-region lasso coefficients beta_mean = colMeans(|beta|) per variable (-important first), flags variables selection cutoff, accepts precomputed beta_fit matrix. print/summary/autoplot companions follow gg_* conventions. gg_sdependent() / plot.gg_sdependent(): tidy wrapper ranked lollipop varPro::sdependent() signal-variable detection. Returns one row per candidate variable (imp_score, graph degree, signal flag) ranked imp_score. Complements gg_udependent() (dependency graph) “variables signal” ranking; shares beta_fit entropy matrix. Follows get.beta.entropy + sdependent workflow varPro::uvarpro() help (iowa-housing example). New uvarpro vignette: short, focused walk-unsupervised varPro wrappers (gg_udependent(), gg_beta_uvarpro(), gg_sdependent()) single uvarpro() fit, using shared beta_fit matrix. three unsupervised sections lifted varpro vignette, now points new one covers five supervised wrappers. Fixed main vignette’s \VignetteIndexEntry, still carried template placeholder “Vignette’s Title” – now reads “Exploring Random Forests ggRandomForests” (index entry CRAN lists, document title, stale one).

title: ggRandomForests v3.3.0
text: gg_partial_varpro(): classification partial plots now default probability. scale = "auto" classification fit resolves "prob" (P(Y = target class)) instead raw log-odds; "odds" "logodds" options. back-transform applied averaging (mean predicted probability). causal contrast shown "logodds". gg_partial_varpro(): survival partial plots now default survival probability. scale = "auto" survival fit resolves "surv" (S(tau | x), bounded 0-1) via new partialpro learner, instead unbounded ensemble-mortality score (still available via scale = "mortality"). "surv" "rmst" default tau median follow-time time omitted – units-safe, data-driven horizon (v3.2.0’s rmst required time; loosening). resolved tau reported message axis label. plot.gg_partial_varpro(): documents causal (virtual-twins) estimator use , explains hidden bounded probability scales. Documentation: plot.gg_partial_varpro() gains “Reading RMST curve” section explaining interpret scale = "rmst" y-axis – RMST(tau) expected event-free time within first tau time-units (area S(t) tau), read model’s time units, bounded tau, higher--better (opposite direction ensemble mortality). also notes tau must supplied fit’s time units, since tau beyond largest event time truncates full restricted mean. code change.

title: ggRandomForests v3.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-23 , 0” varPro::importance() returns degenerate importance table (0 rows, p variables usable z column) – observed intermittently survival fits release-rule step selects variables. now stops clear, specific message explaining empty importance suggesting larger ntree. guard scoped degenerate case ; well-formed fits (survival included) unaffected – blanket survival-family block (cf. reverted #116). Fix: gg_partial_varpro(scale = "rmst", time = tau) now drives survival partial computation instead relabeling y-axis. varPro::partialpro() time argument, default survival learner returns ensemble mortality every horizon – multi-horizon RMST plots built way differed Monte-Carlo noise, tau. scale = "rmst" now passes partialpro() RMST(tau) learner integrates survival curve (integral_0^tau S(t) dt) object$rf, curve genuinely depends tau. path recomputes object (survival fit) part_dta = NULL; precomputed part_dta can relabeled, function now warns try. Also warns tau exceeds model’s event-time range (RMST truncated ) time passed scale ignores . Imports now requires varPro (>= 3.1.0) (version exposing partialpro() learner argument path relies ). Fix: gg_partial_varpro(scale = "surv"/"chf", model = ...) longer errors variable yields empty continuous categorical frame (survival path-C model-label assignment now guards 0-row data.frame). gg_partial_varpro() (gg_partialpro() alias) now forward ... varPro::partialpro() object-driven path. restores control variables computed (xvar.names, nvar) UVT step (cut, nsmp, …) RMST path, must recompute object accept precomputed part_dta. Without explicit xvar.names, partialpro() falls back varPro::get.topvars(object), can return variables fits.

title: ggRandomForests v3.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-13 CRAN fix: skip single test grow trips upstream randomForestSRC gcc-UBSAN report entry.c:184 — unsupervised isolation forest gg_isopro (varPro::isopro(method = "unsupv")). unsupervised grow 0-length yvar.wt, vector rfsrcGrow decrements --bounds pointer; supervised grows unaffected. verified -fsanitize=undefined: every varPro/rfsrc grow test suite, isopro(method = "unsupv") fires entry.c:184. make_iso_fit() therefore calls skip_on_cran() method = "unsupv". ggRandomForests pure R unchanged. broader skip_on_cran() guards added v3.1.1 (varpro, uvarpro, ivarpro, beta.varpro, isopro(method = "rnd") test fixtures) removed: grows supervised (synthetic-supervised) gcc-UBSAN-clean, run CRAN , restoring test coverage. upstream issue fixed randomForestSRC pending CRAN release.

title: ggRandomForests v3.1.1
text: CRAN fix: varPro tests now call skip_on_cran() run CRAN’s check machines, including gcc-UBSAN additional check. triggering upstream randomForestSRC sanitizer issue (0-length array access rfsrcGrow, entry.c:184) surfaces varPro grow (varpro(), beta.varpro(), uvarpro(), isopro(), ivarpro()) builds forest. ggRandomForests pure R code unchanged; varPro tests still run CI (workflows set NOT_CRAN=true) locally; skipped CRAN’s check machines, including gcc-UBSAN check. upstream issue reported randomForestSRC maintainers. varpro vignette now loads every varPro fit precomputed file (vignettes/varpro_precomputed.rds, built vignettes/precompute_varpro.R), vignette performs live varPro grow R CMD check. removes upstream sanitizer path vignette build trims check time. chunk falls back live fit precomputed object absent, vignette remains reproducible source.

title: ggRandomForests v3.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-11 Fix: gg_vimp() single-outcome rfsrc forests now correctly flags variables non-positive VIMP positive column (affecting plot coloring). column named VIMP (uppercase) single-outcome fits flag check accessed $vimp (lowercase), leaving positive stuck TRUE variables. Surfaced Copilot review PR #109. Documentation pass. Deepened varPro-family rfsrc importance/partial/survival help pages upstream randomForestSRC varPro documentation, made line gg_vimp() (permutation, Breiman-Cutler importance) gg_varpro() (varPro release-rule importance) explicit cross-linked. Vignette prose deepened framing; one-line code-comment fixes; fixed stale @return gg_roc() (documented yvar column function return). user-facing behaviour change. Vignettes: regression survival partial-dependence surfaces now rendered static ggplot2 heat maps instead interactive plotly widgets, figures render 96 dpi. cuts installed size ~17 MB ~5 MB (plotly library longer bundled vignette HTML). plotly dropped Suggests. Check time: reduced R CMD check vignette-rebuild test timings bring overall CRAN check comfortably budget (CRAN flagged overall check time 3.1.0 submission). regression survival vignettes use lighter forests (ntree 200 / 150, imputation ntree 100) coarser partial-dependence grids. varpro vignette’s three gg_partial_varpro() calls Boston beta.varpro() fit (~34 s combined) precomputed offline vignettes/precompute_varpro.R loaded vignettes/varpro_precomputed.rds, automatic live-computation fallback file absent. gg_udependent() tests memoise per-fit entropy matrix (varPro::get.beta.entropy(), ~1.5 s pure function fit) instead recomputing per test. user-facing behaviour change.

title: ggRandomForests v3.0.0
text: Version jump 3.0.0. varPro integration major scope expansion plus gg_partialpro() soft-deprecation, major-version territory. Survival / multivariate varPro families, ROC confidence intervals, hazard estimates deferred v3.1.0. CRAN-audit cleanup: gg_brier() / plot.gg_brier() examples move \dontrun \donttest (execute R CMD check ---cran CRAN; library(survival) added Surv() resolves), per-variable message() deprecated surv_partial.rfsrc() removed (one behaviour change: function longer prints line per variable), README points new “varpro” vignette. Fix: importance plots now consistently put -important variable top. gg_varpro(), gg_beta_varpro(), gg_ivarpro() previously built variable factor descending levels, coord_flip() -important variable landed bottom — inverted relative gg_vimp(). three now reverse factor levels match gg_vimp convention (varImpPlot / vip standard). Row order summary() output unchanged (still -important first). new cross-function test pins convention. New vignette: “Exploring variable importance varPro.” Walks full gg_* varPro layer (gg_partial_varpro, gg_varpro, gg_udependent, gg_isopro, gg_beta_varpro, gg_ivarpro) three worked examples — regression (Boston), classification (iris binary + multi-class), survival (PBC). Includes family-support matrix documenting wrapper works forest family. Headline document v3.0.0. gg_ivarpro() plot.gg_ivarpro(): tidy wrapper per-variable-distribution / per-observation-profile plots varPro::ivarpro() (individual / local variable importance) across regression classification (binary + multi-class) families. long-format tidy frame (obs, variable, local_imp, selected) regression; classification adds class column. NA cells filtered sparsity surfaced provenance. which_obs (integer index) collapses single-observation profile; plot switches jittered distribution view horizontal bar chart. which_class (response level name) collapses single class panel; binary fits default last factor level (positive class). cutoff accepts NULL (per-class mean), scalar, named numeric vector — matching gg_beta_varpro classification contract. Optional ivarpro_fit argument lets callers cache expensive ivarpro() call. Last four Phase 4 sub-projects. gg_beta_varpro() adds varPro classification support (binary + multi-class). Binary fits default single positive-class panel (last factor level); multi-class fits return long-format frame class column plot facet_wrap(~ class). Optional which_class selects single class; cutoff accepts scalar per-class named vector. Variables stored factor whose levels set mean(|sum--class-beta|) descending every facet shows rows order. Motivating use case: 30-day mortality. Provenance shape change gg_beta_varpro(): attr(*, "provenance")$cutoff now always named numeric vector — length 1 named "regr" regression, length K named response factor levels classification. Downstream tooling read vector select name; prior scalar shape gone. gg_beta_varpro() plot.gg_beta_varpro(): tidy wrapper default horizontal bar chart varPro::beta.varpro() — per-rule lasso-β refinement variable importance. Aggregates per-rule β̂ variable beta_mean = mean(|β̂|) flags variables selection cutoff (default mean(beta_mean)). Optional beta_fit argument lets callers compute expensive beta.varpro() step reuse result across multiple wrapper calls (different cutoffs, snapshot rebuilds, vignette knits). print / summary / autoplot S3 companions follow existing gg_* conventions. Regression family — classification, regr+, survival tracked Phase 4d (see spec endpoint map). Third three Phase 4 sub-projects. gg_isopro() gains newdata argument fitted varPro::isopro model can score new observations tidy gg_isopro frame. Internally wrapper calls predict.isopro() twice: quantiles = FALSE populate case.depth column (varPro’s native polarity, lower = anomalous) quantiles = TRUE compute howbad = 1 - quantile (wrapper convention, higher = anomalous). polarities visible returned data frame, relationship named roxygen. plot / print / summary / autoplot S3 companions work unchanged new tidy frame; overlay training test scores, bind two extractor calls method label column pass result plot(). Second three Phase 4 sub-projects. Fix (gg_isopro training-path polarity). Bug original gg_isopro (PR #94): varPro’s $howbad isopro fit uses “lower = anomalous” polarity (quantile case.depth), wrapper’s plot method documentation assume “higher = anomalous”. Train scores new test-data scores anti-correlated PR’s training-path flip (howbad = 1 - object$howbad) brought agreement. fix surfaced test-data sanity check (training--newdata top-5 overlap) failed 0/5 instead 5/5 flip. Note: two vdiffr baselines recorded PR #94 (gg-isopro-default gg-isopro-threshold) recorded inverted polarity; visually flipped relative new behaviour CI skips snapshots (VDIFFR_RUN_TESTS = false) failure surfaces. Re-record VDIFFR_RUN_TESTS = true convenient. Documentation: pedagogical pass varPro wrappers (gg_partial_varpro, gg_varpro, gg_udependent plot.* methods). help page now explicit “X ”, “’s output”, “use ” sections reader new varPro can learn underlying method (release rules, beta-entropy dependency, parametric / nonparametric / causal partial estimators) help page alone, just wrapper mechanics. API behavioural change. Documentation: enable roxygen2 markdown package-wide via Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) DESCRIPTION. New roxygen blocks can use backticks [fn()] link syntax; existing \code{} / \link{} markup keeps working. Two source-roxygen edits keep R CMD check clean: randomForest[SRC] R/help.R (markdown read unfinished link) becomes plain randomForestSRC; 95\% escape R/gg_rfsrc.R::bootstrap_survival becomes literal 95%. API rendered-doc behavioural change beyond conventions switch. New gg_isopro() plot.gg_isopro(): tidy wrapper ranked-elbow + density visualisation varPro::isopro isolation-forest anomaly scores. plot.gg_isopro() takes panel = c("", "elbow", "density") optional threshold (score-space) top_n_pct (quantile-space) draw reference line; set, threshold wins message. method column auto-triggers colour grouping multi-method comparisons (use dplyr::bind_rows() three gg_isopro() calls). print / summary / autoplot S3 companions follow existing gg_* conventions. First three Phase 4 sub-projects. plot.gg_variable(): fix render error default multi-class classification plot. default-xvar selection treating yvar (observed-class column) outcome (multi-class pivot facet) predictors; pivoting var dropped column downstream geom_jitter(aes(color = yvar)) referenced, patchwork errored actually rendered. CI catch existing test asserted patchwork class (lazy) snapshots run VDIFFR_RUN_TESTS = false. New test exercises real build every sub-plot. plot.gg_variable(): default-xvar selection used substring grep("time", ...) / grep("event", ...), silently dropped predictor whose name contained substrings – e.g. documented veteran-data survival predictor diagtime. Switch exact matching event / time / yvar / outcome anchored prefix yhat (yhat yhat.<class>). New test exercises diagtime veteran survival forest. New per_class argument, default FALSE. per_class = TRUE forest two classes, gg_roc() returns long-format gg_roc data frame class factor column, plus named AUC vector attribute one entry per class, ordered descending AUC. plot.gg_roc() gains panel = c("overlay", "facet"). object class column, "overlay" colours curves class "facet" gives class panel. summary.gg_roc() prints named per-class AUC values class column present. binary forest, per_class = TRUE nothing, usual single-curve result comes back unchanged. ROC confidence intervals still come, v3.1.0 (issue #7 / #72-CIs). gg_udependent() reads cross-variable dependency scores uvarpro fit, via varPro::get.beta.entropy() varPro::sdependent(). returns tidy list: $edges (variable_from, variable_to, weight), $nodes (variable, degree, selected), $graph, igraph object. plot.gg_udependent() draws dependency network ggraph. Edge width opacity scale dependency strength; node colour marks signal variables. layout configurable ("fr", "kk", "stress", ). ggraph added Suggests:. gg_varpro() pulls per-tree importance scores fitted varpro object draws boxplot per-tree z-score distribution variable. hinges sit 15th 85th percentiles whiskers 5th 95th, box usual Tukey one — reports percentiles actually shows. Variables aggregate z cutoff (default 0.79) colour-highlighted. faithful = TRUE, individual per-tree z-scores jittered box semi-transparent points, white-outlined dot mean, view varPro’s internal bxp output. conditional = TRUE (classification forests ), gg_varpro() reads $conditional.z draws class-conditional importance facet_wrap(~class, nrow=1) bar chart. Set local.std = FALSE allow plot(..., type = "raw"), shows raw per-tree importance instead z-normalised values. classification forest, gg_variable.randomForest() now stores per-class OOB vote fractions yhat.<classname> columns, read object$votes, layout rfsrc path produces. used store single yhat factor column class labels (object$predicted), column shape stopped multi-class pivot plot.gg_variable ever running. vote fractions row-normalised [0, 1], even forest fit norm.votes = FALSE. plot.gg_variable, binary classification: smooth = TRUE x y aesthetics now mapped onto smooth layer correctly. plot.gg_variable, multi-class numeric path: smooth = TRUE now adds smooth layer instead skipping silently. Closes stale issues #81 (fixed PR #83) #82. gg_partial_varpro() takes gg_partialpro() entry point varPro partial dependence plots. accepts optional object argument (originating varpro fit) uses provenance-aware axis labels, scale argument ("auto", "mortality", "rmst", "surv", "chf"). Ensemble mortality labelling (Ishwaran et al. 2008): scale = "mortality", scale = "auto" survival forest, y-axis reads “Ensemble mortality (expected events)”. unbounded relative-risk score, survival probability, documentation says plainly misread. Survival path C: scale = "surv" scale = "chf", gg_partial_varpro() pulls embedded rfsrc forest object$rf returns true S(t) CHF partial curves existing gg_partial_rfsrc machinery. varPro now hard dependency (Imports:). gg_partialpro() soft-deprecated: warns, hands gg_partial_varpro(). removed release v3.0.0. plot.gg_variable() now always returns single ggplot (one variable) patchwork composite (several variables, default) — never bare list. matches v2.7.3 plot.gg_partial* change. list used come back multiple xvar, broke patchwork / autoplot() / layer_data() composition (#80). gg_roc() calc_roc() randomForest now build ROC class probabilities (OOB votes default, honouring oob) rather degenerate three-point curve produced . which_outcome = "" (default gg_roc(rf)) result macro-averaged one-vs-rest ROC, warning. shared .validate_which_outcome helper calc_roc.rfsrc byte--byte unchanged, rfsrc behaviour untouched (#81). Dependency modernization. breaks scripts relied attachment. randomForestSRC randomForest move Depends: Imports:; igraph, callr, varPro added Suggests: (varPro later moves Imports:, first varPro-integration component). library(ggRandomForests) longer puts randomForestSRC randomForest search path. script called rfsrc() randomForest() unqualified library(ggRandomForests) now needs library(randomForestSRC) / library(randomForest), must qualify calls. ggRandomForests unaffected. qualifies every call dependencies.

title: ggRandomForests v2.7.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-12 plot.gg_partial(), plot.gg_partial_rfsrc(), plot.gg_partialpro() now always return single ggplot/patchwork object. Previously, continuous categorical predictors present, returned named list list(continuous=, categorical=), surprised users made autoplot() dispatch ambiguous. two panels now combined vertically via patchwork::wrap_plots() (patchwork moved Suggests Imports). Closes #77. autoplot() S3 methods 10 gg_* classes, delegating corresponding plot.gg_*() method objects work |> pipelines, patchwork, cowplot compositions via ggplot2::autoplot(). print() summary() S3 methods every gg_* data object (gg_error, gg_vimp, gg_rfsrc, gg_variable, gg_partial, gg_partial_rfsrc, gg_partialpro, gg_roc, gg_survival, gg_brier). print() header-— use head() rows. summary() returns printable summary.gg object per-class diagnostics. gg_* constructor now attaches "provenance" attribute (source, family, ntree, n, xvar.names) consumed new methods. New gg_brier() extractor plot.gg_brier() method time-resolved Brier scores CRPS survival forests (issue #9). Wraps randomForestSRC::get.brier.survival() adds mortality-quartile decomposition, 15-85 percent per-subject envelope, running CRPS via trapezoidal integration. Supports cens.model = c("km", "rfsrc"), type = c("brier", "crps"), envelope (overall line + 15-85% ribbon). Multi-model comparison left dplyr::bind_rows() multiple gg_brier outputs — see ?gg_brier example. Visual unification ribbon overlays across plot methods. ribbons now use shared alpha (.gg_ribbon_alpha = 0.2) shared fill (.gg_ribbon_fill = "steelblue") single-series cases (KM/NA CIs, bootstrap CIs, gg_brier envelope); group-stratified ribbons keep group-coloured fill. Statistical bounds unchanged — styling. ggRandomForests v2.7.2 ===================== Add methods references DESCRIPTION (Breiman 2001 Ishwaran et al. 2008, <doi:...> auto-links) per CRAN cookbook. Drop man/shift.Rd Rd file: shift() internal utility example used ggRandomForests:::shift(...). Marked function @noRd longer generates help page. Replace cat() surv_partial.rfsrc() message() progress output suppressible (suppressMessages()) plays nicely inside notebooks / Shiny / quarto. Restore user’s par() settings surv_partial.rfsrc() example via oldpar <- par(.readonly = TRUE); .exit(par(oldpar)).

title: ggRandomForests v2.7.1
text: Fix gg_partial_rfsrc() survival forests: partial.rfsrc() called without partial.type, causing zero-length comparison ((partial.type == "rel.freq") ...) inside C-level prediction routine aborting call. Survival forests now pass partial.type = "surv" (default; configurable via new partial.type argument accepting "surv", "chf", "mort"). unblocks partial-dep chunk survival vignette. Fix gg_partial_rfsrc() survival forests multiple partial.time values: get.partial.plot.data() returns yhat [length(partial.values) x length(partial.time)] matrix, previous code assumed vector crashed column-mismatch assigning time. result now reshaped long form (x, time) pair single row. Improve plot.gg_partial_rfsrc() survival layout: predictor value now x-axis one curve per (rounded) time point coloured Time, faceted variable name. previous default put time x-axis one curve per predictor value, producing saturated legend dozens nearly-identical lines. Add tests/testthat/test_plot_layer_data.R: regression suite uses ggplot2::layer_data() verify plot.gg_*() method renders non-empty layers every supported forest family. Catches empty-figure class bug (transform/plot column-name mismatch) without requiring visual inspection. ggrandomforests.news() now reads NEWS.md (canonical change log R also surfaces via utils::news()). legacy hand-maintained inst/NEWS removed — silently drifted v2.4.0 (June 2025) across three releases, users running helper saw stale version info. One source truth, drift window. Fix plot.gg_vimp() legend duplication: bar geom mapped fill color positive column, fill legend titled “VIMP > 0”, leaving redundant second legend titled “positive”. aesthetics now share “VIMP > 0” title ggplot merges single legend default. Fix plot.gg_vimp() forests -positive VIMP: bar geom previously mapped color (fill), producing hollow / outline- bars “Ignoring unknown labels: fill” warning whenever labs(fill = ...) applied. fill color now mapped unconditionally, bars render filled every case. Add @examples blocks plot.gg_partial_rfsrc() plot.gg_partialpro(). latter uses self-contained mock varpro::partialpro() output structure example runs without pulling varpro dependency.

title: ggRandomForests v2.7.0
text: S3 design overhaul: gg_partial(), gg_partialpro(), gg_partial_rfsrc() now stamp return values S3 classes (gg_partial, gg_partialpro, gg_partial_rfsrc respectively), enabling plot() dispatch without boilerplate. Add plot.gg_partial(), plot.gg_partial_rfsrc(), plot.gg_partialpro() S3 methods; continuous predictors render line plots, categorical bar charts, faceted variable name. Survival forests produce curves time; two-variable surface plots group xvar2.name. Convert gg_survival() S3 generic dispatching class first argument. New gg_survival.rfsrc() method extracts survival response directly fitted forest (separate data argument needed); gg_survival.default() preserves existing interface. Fix plot.gg_survival() auto-coercion: previously called gg_survival(rfsrc_obj) treating forest interval string argument, causing latent crash; replaced inherits() guard. Deprecate surv_partial.rfsrc() via .Deprecated() pointer gg_partial_rfsrc(); package tests updated suppress warning. Fix gg_partial_rfsrc() — make_eval_grid() used unlist(dplyr::select()) coerced factor columns integer codes; now uses newx[[xname]] preserve column class. Categorical detection extended cover .factor() .character() addition cardinality check. Add guards gg_partial_rfsrc(): -NA xval NA removal now emits warning skips variable; -NA grouping variable (xvar2) calls stop(); n_eval cat_limit validated single integers >= 2 near function entry. Fix cyclomatic complexity across gg_partial_rfsrc.R: refactored eight top-level unexported helpers (validate_scalar_int, validate_partial_args, snap_partial_time, make_eval_grid, call_partial_rfsrc, partial_one_var, partial_no_group, partial_with_group, split_partial_result); functions now score cyclocomp_linter limit 20. Fix @param partial.time documentation: “see section ” corrected “see section ”. Replace deprecated tidyr::gather() tidyr::pivot_longer() plot.gg_vimp() plot.gg_partialpro(). Add gg_survival.rfsrc, gg_survival.default, plot.gg_partial, plot.gg_partial_rfsrc, plot.gg_partialpro NAMESPACE; add corresponding @rdname / @export roxygen tags. Update tests: add expect_s3_class() checks new classes; add plot() smoke tests gg_partial, gg_partial_rfsrc, gg_partialpro; add gg_survival.rfsrc tests KM extraction, stratification, error non-survival forest. Add plot.gg_partial, plot.gg_partial_rfsrc, plot.gg_partialpro _pkgdown.yml reference index.

title: ggRandomForests v2.7.0
text: Fix critical visual bug plot.gg_rfsrc: aes() calls used bare string literals instead .data[[col]], causing every aesthetic map constant string rather underlying data column. plot types (regression, classification, survival) affected. Fix aes() bare-string literals plot.gg_roc multi-class branch; remove unreachable (crv < 2) dead-code branch. Fix bootstrap_survival CI-band indexing gg_rfsrc: negative index computed via colnames() -op large datasets latent crash data ≤ 2 unique event times. Fix gg_rfsrc.rfsrc: .null(df[, col]) detect missing columns; replaced !col %% colnames() guard. Fix gg_rfsrc.randomForest: method used non-existent object$xvar; now recovers training frame via .rf_recover_model_frame(). Fix legend suppression plot.gg_error single-outcome forests data frame variable column. Fix gg_vimp plot.gg_vimp: 1:nvar replaced seq_len(nvar) S3 methods; 1:0 silently returned c(1, 0) instead integer(0) nvar == 0. Migrate full test suite testthat 3.x API: expect_is → expect_s3_class / expect_type / expect_true(.*()); expect_equivalent → expect_equal(ignore_attr = TRUE); context() calls removed; testthat 1.x expect_that / is_identical_to removed. Add .lintr package-level linter configuration; fix lintr spacing gg_partial. Improve GitHub Actions: lint.yaml now fails CI lint issue; R-CMD-check.yaml treats warnings errors uses Rtools 44; test-coverage.yaml duplicate codecov upload removed. Add covr vdiffr Suggests.

title: ggRandomForests v1.1.4
text: CRAN release: 2015-03-29 combine.gg_partial bug giving single variable plot.variable object. Remove dplyr depends transitions “Imports” “Suggests”. Argument single outcome gg_vimp plot classification forests. Improvements gg_vimp arguments consistency. Add bootstrap confidence intervals gg_rfsrc function. Initial partial.rfsrc function replace randomForestSRC::plot.variable function. Move cache data randomForestSRC v1.6.1 take advantage rfsrc version checking function calls. Vignette updates JSS submission “ggRandomForests: Exploring Random Forest Survival”. Vignette updates arXiv submission ggRandomForests: Random Forests Regression optimizations reduce package size. Remove tests CRAN build optimise R CMD CHECK times. Remove pdf vignette figure CRAN build. Return S3method calls NAMESPACE “S3 methods exported registered” R V3.2+. Misc Bug Fixes.

title: ggRandomForests v0.2
text: Initial useR!2014 release.
